<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/gen-constants-doc.ts — DO NOT EDIT -->

# TY2025 Tax Constants — Single Source of Truth

## How to Use This Document

- Every numeric constant in the engine is listed with its value, statutory authority, and official URL
- When updating for a new tax year: update this doc FIRST, then update the code to match
- When fact-checking: compare code values against the URLs here — no ad-hoc web searching
- Official sources only: IRS.gov for federal, state DOR .gov sites for state
- Code file references point to `shared/src/constants/` unless otherwise noted

---

## Federal — Income Tax Brackets

**File:** `tax2025.ts` → `TAX_BRACKETS_2025`
**Authority:** IRC §1(a)-(d), (j) — Tax imposed; TCJA §11001 — Rate structure
**Constants:** Rev. Proc. 2024-40, Section 3.01, Table 1 — Taxable income brackets

### Single

| Bracket | Rate |
|------|------|
| $0 – $11,925 | 10% |
| $11,925 – $48,475 | 12% |
| $48,475 – $103,350 | 22% |
| $103,350 – $197,300 | 24% |
| $197,300 – $250,525 | 32% |
| $250,525 – $626,350 | 35% |
| $626,350+ | 37% |

### Married Filing Jointly / QSS

| Bracket | Rate |
|------|------|
| $0 – $23,850 | 10% |
| $23,850 – $96,950 | 12% |
| $96,950 – $206,700 | 22% |
| $206,700 – $394,600 | 24% |
| $394,600 – $501,050 | 32% |
| $501,050 – $751,600 | 35% |
| $751,600+ | 37% |

### Married Filing Separately

| Bracket | Rate |
|------|------|
| $0 – $11,925 | 10% |
| $11,925 – $48,475 | 12% |
| $48,475 – $103,350 | 22% |
| $103,350 – $197,300 | 24% |
| $197,300 – $250,525 | 32% |
| $250,525 – $375,800 | 35% |
| $375,800+ | 37% |

### Head of Household

| Bracket | Rate |
|------|------|
| $0 – $17,000 | 10% |
| $17,000 – $64,850 | 12% |
| $64,850 – $103,350 | 22% |
| $103,350 – $197,300 | 24% |
| $197,300 – $250,500 | 32% |
| $250,500 – $626,350 | 35% |
| $626,350+ | 37% |

## Federal — Standard Deduction

**File:** `tax2025.ts` → `STANDARD_DEDUCTION_2025`, `ADDITIONAL_STANDARD_DEDUCTION`, `DEPENDENT_STANDARD_DEDUCTION`
**Authority:** IRC §63(c) — Standard deduction defined; TCJA §11021 — Increased amounts
**Constants:** Rev. Proc. 2024-40, Section 3.02, Table 5 — Standard deduction amounts

| Filing Status | Amount | Notes |
|------|------|------|
| Single | $15,750 | OBBBA §11021 — increased from $15,000 |
| MFJ / QSS | $31,500 | OBBBA §11021 — increased from $30,000 |
| MFS | $15,750 | OBBBA §11021 — increased from $15,000 |
| HoH | $23,625 | OBBBA §11021 — increased from $22,500 |

| Additional (Age 65+ / Blind) | Amount |
|------|------|
| Unmarried (Single, HoH) | $2,000 |
| Married (MFJ, MFS) | $1,600 |

| Dependent Standard Deduction | Value |
|------|------|
| Minimum amount | $1,350 |
| Earned income + | $450 |

## Federal — Self-Employment Tax

**File:** `tax2025.ts` → `SE_TAX`
**Authority:** IRC §1401(a) — SS rate on SE income; IRC §1401(b) — Medicare rate IRC §1402(a) — Definition of net earnings from SE IRC §3101(b)(2) — Additional Medicare Tax (0.9%)
**Constants:** Rev. Proc. 2024-40 §3 — SS wage base; SSA announcement — $176,100 for 2025

| Constant | Value | Authority |
|------|------|------|
| Total SE rate | 15.3% | IRC §1401(a) 12.4% + IRC §1401(b) 2.9% |
| SS rate | 12.4% | IRC §1401(a) — OASDI (Social Security) rate |
| Medicare rate | 2.9% | IRC §1401(b)(1) — Hospital Insurance (Medicare) rate |
| SS wage base | $176,100 | SSA COLA announcement, Oct 2024 |
| Net earnings factor | 92.35% | IRC §1402(a)(12) — 92.35% of net profit |
| Additional Medicare rate | 0.9% | IRC §3101(b)(2), ACA §9015 — 0.9% additional Medicare tax |
| Add'l Medicare threshold (S/HoH) | $200,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2)(B)(i) — $200k Single/HoH |
| Add'l Medicare threshold (MFJ) | $250,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2)(B)(ii) — $250k MFJ |
| Add'l Medicare threshold (MFS) | $125,000 | IRC §3101(b)(2)(B)(iii) — $125k MFS |
| Minimum SE earnings | $400 | IRC §1402(b) — SE tax applies only if net SE earnings ≥ $400 |

## Federal — Capital Gains / Qualified Dividends

**File:** `tax2025.ts` → `CAPITAL_GAINS_RATES`
**Authority:** IRC §1(h) — Preferential rates for net capital gain and qualified dividends IRC §1(h)(1)(B)-(D) — 0%/15%/20% rate tiers
**Constants:** Rev. Proc. 2024-40, Section 3.12 — Capital gain rate thresholds

### 0% Rate Upper Thresholds

| Filing Status | Threshold |
|------|------|
| Single | $48,350 |
| MFJ / QSS | $96,700 |
| MFS | $48,350 |
| HoH | $64,750 |

### 15% Rate Upper Thresholds

| Filing Status | Threshold |
|------|------|
| Single | $533,400 |
| MFJ / QSS | $600,050 |
| MFS | $300,025 |
| HoH | $566,700 |

Above the 15% threshold: **20%** rate applies.
Unrecaptured §1250 gain: **25%** rate.

## Federal — Self-Employed Health Insurance (Form 7206)

**File:** `tax2025.ts` → `LTC_PREMIUM_LIMITS_2025`
**Authority:** IRC §162(l) — Self-employed health insurance deduction; IRC §213(d)(10) — Eligible long-term care premiums (age-based limits)
**Constants:** Rev. Proc. 2024-40 — 2025 inflation-adjusted LTC premium limits

### Long-Term Care Premium Limits (per person)

| Age at End of Tax Year | Deductible Limit |
|------|------|
| 40 or under | $480 |
| 41 to 50 | $900 |
| 51 to 60 | $1,800 |
| 61 to 70 | $4,810 |
| 71 and over | $6,020 |

**Note:** These are per-person limits. For MFJ filers, each spouse's LTC premiums are capped independently based on their age, then summed. Used by Form 7206 (`form7206.ts`) to cap deductible long-term care insurance premiums within the self-employed health insurance deduction.

---

| FPL % Range | Initial % | Final % |

| 0-150% | 0% | 0% |
| 150-200% | 0% | 2% |
| 200-250% | 2% | 4% |
| 250-300% | 4% | 6% |
| 300-400% | 6% | 8.5% |
| 400%+ | 8.5% | 8.5% |

| Phase-out start (S/HoH/MFS) | $100,000 |
| Phase-out start (MFJ) | $200,000 |

### Enhanced Senior Deduction (§104)

| Constant | Value |
|------|------|
| Amount per qualifying individual (65+) | $6,000 |
| Phase-out start (S/HoH) | $75,000 |
| Phase-out start (MFJ) | $150,000 |
| Phase-out rate | 6% of excess MAGI |

**File:** `tax2025.ts`

### Home Office (`HOME_OFFICE`)
**Authority:** IRC §280A(c) — Home office deduction requirements
| Constant | Value |
|------|------|
| Simplified rate | $5/sq ft |
| Max sq ft | 300 |
| Max deduction | $1,500 |

### Vehicle Mileage (`VEHICLE`)
**Authority:** IRC §162 — Business expenses; IRC §274(d) — Substantiation requirements
| Constant | Value |
|------|------|
| Standard mileage rate | $0.70/mile |

### Schedule H — Household Employee Tax
**Authority:** IRC §3111 — Employer FICA; IRC §3301 — FUTA tax IRC §3121(x) — Domestic service employment threshold
| Constant | Value |
|------|------|
| Cash wage threshold | $2,800 |
| FUTA quarterly threshold | $1,000 |
| SS wage base | $176,100 |
| FUTA net rate | 0.6% |

### Schedule R — Credit for Elderly/Disabled
**Authority:** IRC §30C — Alternative fuel vehicle refueling property credit IRA §13404 — Extension and modification (through 2032) IRC §22 — Credit for the elderly and the permanently and totally disabled
| Filing Status | Initial Amount | AGI Threshold |
|------|------|------|
| Single/HoH/QSS | $5,000 | $7,500 |
| MFJ (both qualifying) | $7,500 | $10,000 |
| MFJ (one qualifying) | $5,000 | $10,000 |
| MFS | $3,750 | $5,000 |
Credit rate: 15%

### Estimated Tax Penalty
**Authority:** IRC §6654 — Failure to pay estimated income tax IRC §6621(a)(2) — Underpayment rate
| Constant | Value |
|------|------|
| Underpayment rate | 7% annual |
| Required payment | 90% of current year |
| Safe harbor (prior year) | 100% |
| Safe harbor (high income) | 110% if AGI > $150,000 |
| Minimum penalty threshold | $1,000 |

## State — No-Tax States (9)

**File:** `engine/state/stateRegistry.ts`

These states impose no individual income tax (or tax only interest/dividends):

| State | Code | Notes |
|------|------|------|
| Alaska | AK | No income tax |
| Florida | FL | No income tax |
| Nevada | NV | No income tax |
| New Hampshire | NH | Interest/dividends tax repealed as of 2025 |
| South Dakota | SD | No income tax |
| Tennessee | TN | Hall Tax repealed as of 2021 |
| Texas | TX | No income tax |
| Washington | WA | No income tax (7% cap gains tax on >$270k is separate) |
| Wyoming | WY | No income tax |

| Rate | Single | MFJ |

| 1% | $0 – $10,756 | $0 – $21,512 |
| 2% | $10,756 – $25,499 | $21,512 – $50,998 |
| 4% | $25,499 – $40,245 | $50,998 – $80,490 |
| 6% | $40,245 – $55,866 | $80,490 – $111,732 |
| 8% | $55,866 – $70,612 | $111,732 – $141,224 |
| 9.3% | $70,612 – $360,659 | $141,224 – $721,318 |
| 10.3% | $360,659 – $432,790 | $721,318 – $865,580 |
| 11.3% | $432,790 – $721,314 | $865,580 – $1,442,628 |
| 12.3% | $721,314+ | $1,442,628+ |

Std. deduction: $5,540 (S) / $11,080 (MFJ). Exemption credit: $153 (S) / $306 (MFJ). Dependent: $475. + 1% Mental Health Services Tax (Prop 63) above $1,000,000. CalEITC: 85% match rate (earned income < $30,950).

| $150,000 – $250,000 | 5.5% |
| $250,000+ | 5.75% |

Std. deduction: 15% of MD AGI (min $1,800/$3,600, max $2,550/$5,150). Personal exemption: $3,200/person. County piggyback tax: 2.25%-3.2% depending on county. EITC: 45% refundable.

### Alabama
**File:** `constants/states/al.ts`
**Authority:** Ala. Code §40-18-5, §40-18-15, §40-18-19
**URL:** https://www.revenue.alabama.gov/individual-corporate/taxes-individual-income-tax/

| Rate | Single | MFJ |
|------|------|------|
| 2% | $0 – $500 | $0 – $1,000 |
| 4% | $500 – $3,000 | $1,000 – $6,000 |
| 5% | $3,000+ | $6,000+ |

Std. deduction: $3,000 (S) / $8,500 (MFJ) / $4,250 (HoH). Personal exemption: $1,500 (S) / $3,000 (MFJ). Dependent: $1,000. **Unique: deducts federal tax paid.** SS fully exempt. No state EITC.

### Hawaii
**File:** `constants/states/hi.ts`
**Authority:** HRS §235-51, §235-54, §235-55.85, §235-110.91; Act 45/46 SLH 2024
**URL:** https://tax.hawaii.gov/forms/a1_b1_5slsincome/

| Rate | Single | MFJ |
|------|------|------|
| 1.4% | $0 – $9,600 | $0 – $19,200 |
| 3.2% | $9,600 – $14,400 | $19,200 – $28,800 |
| 5.5% | $14,400 – $19,200 | $28,800 – $38,400 |
| 6.4% | $19,200 – $24,000 | $38,400 – $48,000 |
| 6.8% | $24,000 – $36,000 | $48,000 – $72,000 |
| 7.2% | $36,000 – $48,000 | $72,000 – $96,000 |
| 7.6% | $48,000 – $125,000 | $96,000 – $250,000 |
| 7.9% | $125,000 – $175,000 | $250,000 – $350,000 |
| 8.25% | $175,000 – $225,000 | $350,000 – $450,000 |
| 9% | $225,000 – $275,000 | $450,000 – $550,000 |
| 10% | $275,000 – $325,000 | $550,000 – $650,000 |
| 11% | $325,000+ | $650,000+ |

Std. deduction: $4,400 (S) / $8,800 (MFJ) / $6,424 (HoH). Personal exemption: $1,144/person. Dependent: $1,144. EITC: 20% refundable. Food/excise credit: $110/exemption.
